relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Now the honored one is laughed at by the young.
Those he once regarded as useless now mock him.
They come from hunger, want, and desolation.
They survive on harsh plants and roots.
They are driven out like thieves.
They dwell in ravines, dust, and rock holes.
They huddle among bushes and nettles.
Nameless and despised, they were pushed from the land.
Now Job has become their mocking song.
They abhor him and shame his face.
Because Job has been humbled, they cast off restraint.
The rabble rises and builds roads of ruin against him.
They break up his path and deepen his calamity.
They roll in through the breach.
Terror chases away dignity, and salvation passes like a cloud.
Job’s soul pours out as affliction seizes him.
Night enters his bones with unresting pain.
Even his garment feels distorted and binding.
He feels thrown into mire, dust, and ashes.
He cries, but no answer comes.
The Divine feels turned against him.
He is lifted into wind and dissolved in storm.
He knows death waits as the house of all living.
Still, the ruined stretch out a hand and cry for help.
Job remembers his own compassion for the hard-pressed and needy.
He hoped for good, but harm came; he waited for light, but darkness arrived.
His inward life boils without rest.
He walks darkened and cries in the assembly.
He feels kinship with jackals and ostriches.
His skin and bones carry visible anguish.
Music has turned into mourning.
